$comment: Generated from types/actions.ts — do not edit
title: AHP Action Types
description: All action types in the Agent Host Protocol.

IActionOrigin.description: Identifies the client that originally dispatched an action.

IActionEnvelope.description: Every action is wrapped in an `ActionEnvelope`.

IToolCallActionBase.description:
Base interface for all tool-call-scoped actions, carrying the common
session, turn, and tool call identifiers.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

IRootAgentsChangedAction.description: Fired when available agent backends or their models change.

IRootActiveSessionsChangedAction.description: Fired when the number of active sessions changes.

IRootTerminalsChangedAction.description:
Fired when the list of known terminals changes.

Full-replacement semantics: the `terminals` array replaces the previous
`terminals` entirely.

ISessionTurnStartedAction.description: User sent a message; server starts agent processing.

queuedMessageId.description: If this turn was auto-started from a queued message, the ID of that message

ISessionDeltaAction.description:
Streaming text chunk from the assistant, appended to a specific response part.

The server MUST first emit a `session/responsePart` to create the target
part (markdown or reasoning), then use this action to append text to it.

partId.description: Identifier of the response part to append to

ISessionResponsePartAction.description: Structured content appended to the response.

ISessionToolCallStartAction.description:
A tool call begins — parameters are streaming from the LM.

For client-provided tools, the server sets `toolClientId` to identify the
owning client. That client is responsible for executing the tool once it
reaches the `running` state and dispatching `session/toolCallComplete`.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

ISessionToolCallDeltaAction.description: Streaming partial parameters for a tool call.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

ISessionToolCallReadyAction.description:
Tool call parameters are complete, or a running tool requires re-confirmation.

When dispatched for a `streaming` tool call, transitions to `pending-confirmation`
or directly to `running` if `confirmed` is set.

When dispatched for a `running` tool call (e.g. mid-execution permission needed),
transitions back to `pending-confirmation`. The `invocationMessage` and `_meta`
SHOULD be updated to describe the specific confirmation needed. Clients use the
standard `session/toolCallConfirmed` flow to approve or deny.

For client-provided tools, the server typically sets `confirmed` to
`'not-needed'` so the tool transitions directly to `running`, where the
owning client can begin execution immediately.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do or what confirmation is needed

confirmationTitle.description: Short title for the confirmation prompt (e.g. `"Run in terminal"`, `"Write file"`)

confirmed.description: If set, the tool was auto-confirmed and transitions directly to `running`

ISessionToolCallApprovedAction.description: Client approves a pending tool call. The tool transitions to `running`.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

ISessionToolCallDeniedAction.description:
Client denies a pending tool call. The tool transitions to `cancelled`.

For client-provided tools, the owning client MUST dispatch this if it does
not recognize the tool or cannot execute it.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

userSuggestion.description: What the user suggested doing instead

ISessionToolCallCompleteAction.description:
Tool execution finished. Transitions to `completed` or `pending-result-confirmation`
if `requiresResultConfirmation` is `true`.

For client-provided tools (where `toolClientId` is set on the tool call state),
the owning client dispatches this action with the execution result. The server
SHOULD reject this action if the dispatching client does not match `toolClientId`.

Servers waiting on a client tool call MAY time out after a reasonable duration
if the implementing client disconnects or becomes unresponsive, and dispatch
this action with `result.success = false` and an appropriate error.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

requiresResultConfirmation.description: If true, the result requires client approval before finalizing

ISessionToolCallResultConfirmedAction.description:
Client approves or denies a tool's result.

If `approved` is `false`, the tool transitions to `cancelled` with reason `result-denied`.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

ISessionToolCallContentChangedAction.description:
Partial content produced while a tool is still executing.

Replaces the `content` array on the running tool call state. Clients can
use this to display live feedback (e.g. a terminal reference) before the
tool completes.

For client-provided tools (where `toolClientId` is set on the tool call state),
the owning client dispatches this action to stream intermediate content while
executing. The server SHOULD reject this action if the dispatching client does
not match `toolClientId`.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

content.description: The current partial content for the running tool call

ISessionTurnCompleteAction.description: Turn finished — the assistant is idle.

ISessionTurnCancelledAction.description: Turn was aborted; server stops processing.

ISessionTitleChangedAction.description:
Session title updated. Fired by the server when the title is auto-generated
from conversation, or dispatched by a client to rename a session.

ISessionUsageAction.description: Token usage report for a turn.

ISessionReasoningAction.description:
Reasoning/thinking text from the model, appended to a specific reasoning response part.

The server MUST first emit a `session/responsePart` to create the target
reasoning part, then use this action to append text to it.

partId.description: Identifier of the reasoning response part to append to

ISessionIsReadChangedAction.description:
The read state of the session changed.

Dispatched by a client to mark a session as read (e.g. after viewing it)
or unread (e.g. after new activity since the client last looked at it).

isRead.description: Whether the session has been read

ISessionIsDoneChangedAction.description:
The done state of the session changed.

Dispatched by a client to mark a session as done (e.g. the task is
complete) or to reopen it.

ISessionDiffsChangedAction.description:
The file diffs for the session changed.

Full-replacement semantics: the `diffs` array replaces the previous
`summary.diffs` entirely.

diffs.description: Updated file diffs for the session

ISessionServerToolsChangedAction.description:
Server tools for this session have changed.

Full-replacement semantics: the `tools` array replaces the previous `serverTools` entirely.

ISessionActiveClientChangedAction.description:
The active client for this session has changed.

A client dispatches this action with its own `ISessionActiveClient` to claim
the active role, or with `null` to release it. The server SHOULD reject if
another client is already active. The server SHOULD automatically dispatch
this action with `activeClient: null` when the active client disconnects.

activeClient.description: The new active client, or `null` to unset

ISessionActiveClientToolsChangedAction.description:
The active client's tool list has changed.

Full-replacement semantics: the `tools` array replaces the active client's
previous tools entirely. The server SHOULD reject if the dispatching client
is not the current active client.

ISessionCustomizationsChangedAction.description:
The session's customizations have changed.

Full-replacement semantics: the `customizations` array replaces the
previous `customizations` entirely.

ISessionCustomizationToggledAction.description:
A client toggled a customization on or off.

The server locates the customization by `uri` in the session's
customization list and sets its `enabled` flag.

uri.description: The URI of the customization to toggle

enabled.description: Whether to enable or disable the customization

ISessionConfigChangedAction.description:
Client changed a mutable config value mid-session.

Only properties with `sessionMutable: true` in the config schema may be
changed. The server validates and broadcasts the action; the reducer merges
the new values into `state.config.values`.

config.description: Updated config values (merged into existing config)

ISessionTruncatedAction.description:
Truncates a session's history. If `turnId` is provided, all turns after that
turn are removed and the specified turn is kept. If `turnId` is omitted, all
turns are removed.

If there is an active turn it is silently dropped and the session status
returns to `idle`.

Common use-case: truncate old data then dispatch a new
`session/turnStarted` with an edited message.

turnId.description: Keep turns up to and including this turn. Omit to clear all turns.

ISessionPendingMessageSetAction.description:
A pending message was set (upsert semantics: creates or replaces).

For steering messages, this always replaces the single steering message.
For queued messages, if a message with the given `id` already exists it is
updated in place; otherwise it is appended to the queue. If the session is
idle when a queued message is set, the server SHOULD immediately consume it
and start a new turn.

kind.description: Whether this is a steering or queued message

id.description: Unique identifier for this pending message

ISessionPendingMessageRemovedAction.description:
A pending message was removed (steering or queued).

Dispatched by clients to cancel a pending message, or by the server when
it consumes a message (e.g. starting a turn from a queued message or
injecting a steering message into the current turn).

kind.description: Whether this is a steering or queued message

id.description: Identifier of the pending message to remove

ISessionQueuedMessagesReorderedAction.description:
Reorder the queued messages.

The `order` array contains the IDs of queued messages in their new
desired order. IDs not present in the current queue are ignored.
Queued messages whose IDs are absent from `order` are appended at
the end in their original relative order (so a client with a stale
view of the queue never silently drops messages).

order.description: Queued message IDs in the desired order

ISessionInputRequestedAction.description:
A session requested input from the user.

Full-request upsert semantics: the `request` replaces any existing request
with the same `id`, or is appended if it is new. Answer drafts are preserved
unless `request.answers` is provided.

request.description: Input request to create or replace

ISessionInputAnswerChangedAction.description:
A client updated, submitted, skipped, or removed a single in-progress answer.

Dispatching with `answer: undefined` removes that question's answer draft.

questionId.description: Question identifier within the input request

answer.description: Updated answer, or `undefined` to clear an answer draft

ISessionInputCompletedAction.description:
A client accepted, declined, or cancelled a session input request.

If accepted, the server uses `answers` (when provided) plus the request's
synced answer state to resume the blocked operation.

answers.description: Optional final answer replacement, keyed by question ID

ITerminalDataAction.description:
Terminal output data (pty → client direction).

Appends `data` to the terminal's `content` in the reducer.

`terminal/data` and `terminal/input` are intentionally separate actions
because standard write-ahead reconciliation is not safe for terminal I/O.
A pty is a stateful, mutable process — optimistically applying input or
predicting output would produce incorrect state. Instead, `terminal/input`
is a side-effect-only action (client → server → pty), and `terminal/data`
is server-authoritative output (pty → server → client).

data.description: Output data (may contain ANSI escape sequences)

ITerminalInputAction.description:
Keyboard input sent to the terminal process (client → pty direction).

This is a side-effect-only action: the server forwards the data to the
terminal's pty. The reducer treats this as a no-op since `terminal/data`
actions will reflect any resulting output.

See `terminal/data` for why these two actions are kept separate.

data.description: Input data to send to the pty

ITerminalResizedAction.description:
Terminal dimensions changed.

Dispatchable by clients to request a resize, or by the server to inform
clients of the actual terminal dimensions.

ITerminalClaimedAction.description:
Terminal claim changed. A client or session transfers ownership of the terminal.

The server SHOULD reject if the dispatching client does not currently hold
the claim.

ITerminalTitleChangedAction.description:
Terminal title changed.

Fired by the server when the terminal process updates its title (e.g. via
escape sequences), or dispatched by a client to rename a terminal.

exitCode.description: Process exit code. `undefined` if the process was killed without an exit code.

ITerminalCommandDetectionAvailableAction.description:
Shell integration has loaded and the terminal now supports command
detection. The server dispatches this when shell integration becomes
available (which may happen asynchronously after the terminal is created).

Clients MUST NOT assume command detection is available until this action
(or `terminal/commandExecuted`) has been received.

ITerminalCommandExecutedAction.description:
A command has been submitted to the shell and is now executing.
All subsequent `terminal/data` actions (until the matching
`terminal/commandFinished`) constitute this command's output.

commandId.description:
Stable identifier for this command, scoped to the terminal URI.
Allows correlating `commandExecuted` → `commandFinished` pairs.

commandLine.description: The command line text that was submitted

timestamp.description:
Unix timestamp (ms) of when the command started executing, as measured
on the server.

ITerminalCommandFinishedAction.description:
A command has finished executing.

The sequence of `terminal/data` actions between the preceding
`terminal/commandExecuted` (same `commandId`) and this action constitutes
the complete output of the command.

exitCode.description: Shell exit code. `undefined` if the shell did not report one.

durationMs.description:
Wall-clock duration of the command in milliseconds, as measured by the
shell integration script on the server side.

ISessionToolCallConfirmedAction.description: Client confirms or denies a pending tool call.

Icon.description: An optionally-sized icon that can be displayed in a user interface.

src.description:
A standard URI pointing to an icon resource. May be an HTTP/HTTPS URL or a
`data:` URI with Base64-encoded image data.

Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs serving icons are from the
same domain as the client/server or a trusted domain.

Consumers SHOULD take appropriate precautions when consuming SVGs as they can contain
executable JavaScript.

contentType.description:
Optional MIME type override if the source MIME type is missing or generic.
For example: `"image/png"`, `"image/jpeg"`, or `"image/svg+xml"`.

sizes.description:
Optional array of strings that specify sizes at which the icon can be used.
Each string should be in WxH format (e.g., `"48x48"`, `"96x96"`) or `"any"` for scalable formats like SVG.

If not provided, the client should assume that the icon can be used at any size.

theme.description:
Optional specifier for the theme this icon is designed for. `"light"` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a light background, and `"dark"` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a dark background.

If not provided, the client should assume the icon can be used with any theme.

IProtectedResourceMetadata.description:
Describes a protected resource's authentication requirements using
[RFC 9728](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728) (OAuth 2.0
Protected Resource Metadata) semantics.

Field names use snake_case to match the RFC 9728 JSON format.

resource.description:
REQUIRED. The protected resource's resource identifier, a URL using the
`https` scheme with no fragment component (e.g. `"https://api.github.com"`).

resource_name.description: OPTIONAL. Human-readable name of the protected resource.

authorization_servers.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of OAuth authorization server identifier URLs.

jwks_uri.description: OPTIONAL. URL of the protected resource's JWK Set document.

scopes_supported.description: RECOMMENDED. JSON array of OAuth 2.0 scope values used in authorization requests.

bearer_methods_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of Bearer Token presentation methods supported.

resource_signing_alg_values_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of JWS signing algorithms supported.

resource_encryption_alg_values_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of JWE encryption algorithms (alg) supported.

resource_encryption_enc_values_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of JWE encryption algorithms (enc) supported.

resource_documentation.description: OPTIONAL. URL of human-readable documentation for the resource.

resource_policy_uri.description: OPTIONAL. URL of the resource's data-usage policy.

resource_tos_uri.description: OPTIONAL. URL of the resource's terms of service.

required.description:
AHP extension. Whether authentication is required for this resource.

- `true` (default) — the agent cannot be used without a valid token.
  The server SHOULD return `AuthRequired` (`-32007`) if the client
  attempts to use the agent without authenticating.
- `false` — the agent works without authentication but MAY offer
  enhanced capabilities when a token is provided.

Clients SHOULD treat an absent field the same as `true`.

IRootState.description: Global state shared with every client subscribed to `agenthost:/root`.

agents.description: Available agent backends and their models

activeSessions.description: Number of active (non-disposed) sessions on the server

terminals.description: Known terminals on the server. Subscribe to individual terminal URIs for full state.

protectedResources.description:
Protected resources this agent requires authentication for.

Each entry describes an OAuth 2.0 protected resource using
[RFC 9728](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728) semantics.
Clients should obtain tokens from the declared `authorization_servers`
and push them via the `authenticate` command before creating sessions
with this agent.

customizations.description:
Customizations (Open Plugins) associated with this agent.

Each entry is a reference to an [Open Plugins](https://open-plugins.com/)
plugin that the agent host can activate for sessions using this agent.

IPendingMessage.description:
A message queued for future delivery to the agent.

Steering messages are injected into the current turn mid-flight.
Queued messages are automatically started as new turns after the
current turn naturally finishes.

id.description: Unique identifier for this pending message

ISessionState.description: Full state for a single session, loaded when a client subscribes to the session's URI.

serverTools.description: Tools provided by the server (agent host) for this session

activeClient.description: The client currently providing tools and interactive capabilities to this session

steeringMessage.description: Message to inject into the current turn at a convenient point

queuedMessages.description: Messages to send automatically as new turns after the current turn finishes

inputRequests.description: Requests for user input that are currently blocking or informing session progress

config.description: Session configuration schema and current values

customizations.description:
Server-provided customizations active in this session.

Client-provided customizations are available on
{@link ISessionActiveClient.customizations | activeClient.customizations}.

ISessionActiveClient.description:
The client currently providing tools and interactive capabilities to a session.

Only one client may be active per session at a time. The server SHOULD
automatically unset the active client if that client disconnects.

tools.description: Tools this client provides to the session

customizations.description: Customizations this client contributes to the session

ISessionFileDiff.description: A summary of changes to a single file within a session.

added.description: Number of items added (e.g., lines for text files, cells for notebooks)

removed.description: Number of items removed (e.g., lines for text files, cells for notebooks)

IProjectInfo.description: Server-owned project metadata for a session.

workingDirectory.description: The working directory URI for this session

isRead.description: Whether the client has viewed this session since its last modification

isDone.description: Whether the session has been marked as done by the client

diffs.description: Files changed during this session with diff statistics

ISessionConfigPropertySchema.description:
A JSON Schema-compatible string enum property descriptor with display extensions.

Standard JSON Schema fields (`type`, `title`, `description`, `default`,
`enum`) allow validators to process the schema. Display extensions
(`enumLabels`, `enumDescriptions`) are parallel arrays that provide UI metadata for each `enum` value.

type.description: JSON Schema: property type. Only string enum properties are currently supported.

title.description: JSON Schema: human-readable label for the property

enumLabels.description: Display extension: human-readable label per enum value (parallel array)

enumDescriptions.description: Display extension: description per enum value (parallel array)

enumDynamic.description:
Display extension: when `true`, the full set of allowed values is too large
to enumerate statically. The client SHOULD use `sessionConfigCompletions`
to fetch matching values based on user input. Any values in `enum` are
seed/recent values for initial display.

readOnly.description: JSON Schema: when `true`, the property is displayed but cannot be modified by the user

sessionMutable.description: When `true`, the user may change this property after session creation

ISessionConfigSchema.description: A JSON Schema object describing available session configuration metadata.

properties.description: JSON Schema: property descriptors keyed by property id

required.description: JSON Schema: list of required property ids

ISessionConfigState.description:
Live session configuration metadata.

The schema describes the available configuration properties and the values
contain the current value for each resolved property.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing available configuration properties

ISessionInputOption.description: A choice in a select-style question.

id.description: Stable option identifier; for MCP enum values this is the enum string

recommended.description: Whether this option is the recommended/default choice

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

ISessionInputTextQuestion.description: Text question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

format.description: Format hint for text questions, such as `email`, `uri`, `date`, or `date-time`

ISessionInputNumberQuestion.description: Numeric question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

ISessionInputBooleanQuestion.description: Boolean question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

ISessionInputSingleSelectQuestion.description: Single-select question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

options.description: Options the user may select from

allowFreeformInput.description: Whether the user may enter text instead of selecting an option

ISessionInputMultiSelectQuestion.description: Multi-select question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

options.description: Options the user may select from

allowFreeformInput.description: Whether the user may enter text in addition to selecting options

ISessionInputRequest.description:
A live request for user input.

The server creates or replaces requests with `session/inputRequested`.
Clients sync drafts with `session/inputAnswerChanged` and complete requests
with `session/inputCompleted`.

message.description: Display message for the request as a whole

url.description: URL the user should review or open, for URL-style elicitations

questions.description: Ordered questions to ask the user

answers.description: Current draft or submitted answers, keyed by question ID

freeformValues.description: Free-form text entered instead of selecting an option
Free-form text entered in addition to selected options
Free-form reason or value captured while skipping, if any

responseParts.description:
All response content in stream order: text, tool calls, reasoning, and content refs.

Consumers should derive display text by concatenating markdown parts,
and find tool calls by filtering for `ToolCall` parts.

IActiveTurn.description: An in-progress turn — the assistant is actively streaming.

responseParts.description:
All response content in stream order: text, tool calls, reasoning, and content refs.

Tool call parts include `pendingPermissions` when permissions are awaiting user approval.

id.description: Part identifier, used by `session/delta` to target this part for content appends

IContentRef.description: A reference to large content stored outside the state tree.

IResourceReponsePart.description: A content part that's a reference to large content stored outside the state tree.

IToolCallResponsePart.description:
A tool call represented as a response part.

Tool calls are part of the response stream, interleaved with text and
reasoning. The `toolCall.toolCallId` serves as the part identifier for
actions that target this part.

id.description: Part identifier, used by `session/reasoning` to target this part for content appends

IToolCallBase.description: Metadata common to all tool call states.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

IToolCallParameterFields.description: Properties available once tool call parameters are fully received.

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

IToolCallResult.description: Tool execution result details, available after execution completes.

pastTenseMessage.description: Past-tense description of what the tool did

content.description:
Unstructured result content blocks.

This mirrors the `content` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

structuredContent.description:
Optional structured result object.

This mirrors the `structuredContent` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

error.description: Error details if the tool failed

IToolCallStreamingState.description: LM is streaming the tool call parameters.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Progress message shown while parameters are streaming

IToolCallPendingConfirmationState.description:
Parameters are complete, or a running tool requires re-confirmation
(e.g. a mid-execution permission check).

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

confirmationTitle.description: Short title for the confirmation prompt (e.g. `"Run in terminal"`, `"Write file"`)

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

confirmed.description: How the tool was confirmed for execution

content.description:
Partial content produced while the tool is still executing.

For example, a terminal content block lets clients subscribe to live
output before the tool completes.

IToolCallPendingResultConfirmationState.description: Tool finished executing, waiting for client to approve the result.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

pastTenseMessage.description: Past-tense description of what the tool did

content.description:
Unstructured result content blocks.

This mirrors the `content` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

structuredContent.description:
Optional structured result object.

This mirrors the `structuredContent` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

error.description: Error details if the tool failed

confirmed.description: How the tool was confirmed for execution

IToolCallCompletedState.description: Tool completed successfully or with an error.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

pastTenseMessage.description: Past-tense description of what the tool did

content.description:
Unstructured result content blocks.

This mirrors the `content` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

structuredContent.description:
Optional structured result object.

This mirrors the `structuredContent` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

error.description: Error details if the tool failed

confirmed.description: How the tool was confirmed for execution

IToolCallCancelledState.description: Tool call was cancelled before execution.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

userSuggestion.description: What the user suggested doing instead

IToolDefinition.description: Describes a tool available in a session, provided by either the server or the active client.

description.description: Description of what the tool does

inputSchema.description:
JSON Schema defining the expected input parameters.

Optional because client-provided tools may not have formal schemas.
Mirrors MCP `Tool.inputSchema`.

outputSchema.description:
JSON Schema defining the structure of the tool's output.

Mirrors MCP `Tool.outputSchema`.

annotations.description: Behavioral hints about the tool. All properties are advisory.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata.

Mirrors the MCP `_meta` convention.

IToolAnnotations.description:
Behavioral hints about a tool. All properties are advisory and not
guaranteed to faithfully describe tool behavior.

Mirrors MCP `ToolAnnotations` from the Model Context Protocol specification.

readOnlyHint.description: Tool does not modify its environment (default: false)

destructiveHint.description: Tool may perform destructive updates (default: true)

idempotentHint.description: Repeated calls with the same arguments have no additional effect (default: false)

openWorldHint.description: Tool may interact with external entities (default: true)

IToolResultTextContent.description:
Text content in a tool result.

Mirrors MCP `TextContent`.

IToolResultEmbeddedResourceContent.description:
Base64-encoded binary content embedded in a tool result.

Mirrors MCP `EmbeddedResource` for inline binary data.

IToolResultResourceContent.description:
A reference to a resource stored outside the tool result.

Wraps {@link IContentRef} for lazy-loading large results.

IToolResultFileEditContent.description:
Describes a file modification performed by a tool.

Supports creates (only `after`), deletes (only `before`), renames/moves
(different `uri` in `before` and `after`), and edits (same `uri`, different content).

before.description: The file state before the edit. Absent for file creations or for in-place file edits.

after.description: The file state after the edit. Absent for file deletions.

IToolResultTerminalContent.description:
A reference to a terminal whose output is relevant to this tool result.

Clients can subscribe to the terminal's URI to stream its output in real
time, providing live feedback while a tool is executing.

resource.description: Terminal URI (subscribable for full terminal state)

title.description: Display title for the terminal content

IToolResultSubagentContent.description:
A reference to a subagent session spawned by a tool.

Clients can subscribe to the subagent's session URI to stream its
progress in real time, including inner tool calls and responses.

resource.description: Subagent session URI (subscribable for full session state)

description.description: Human-readable description of the subagent's task

ICustomizationRef.description:
A reference to an [Open Plugins](https://open-plugins.com/) plugin.

This is intentionally thin — AHP specifies plugin identity and metadata
but not implementation details, which are defined by the Open Plugins spec.

uri.description: Plugin URI (e.g. an HTTPS URL or marketplace identifier)

description.description: Description of what the plugin provides

nonce.description:
Opaque version token for this customization.

Clients SHOULD include a nonce with every customization they provide.
Consumers can compare nonces to detect whether a customization has
changed since it was last seen, avoiding redundant reloads or copies.

ISessionCustomization.description:
A customization active in a session.

Entries without a `clientId` are server-provided; entries with a `clientId`
originate from that client.

customization.description: The plugin this customization refers to

enabled.description: Whether this customization is currently enabled

statusMessage.description: Human-readable status detail (e.g. error message or degradation warning).

ITerminalInfo.description: Lightweight terminal metadata exposed on the root state.

resource.description: Terminal URI (subscribable for full terminal state)

exitCode.description: Process exit code, if the terminal process has exited

ITerminalClientClaim.description: A terminal claimed by a connected client.

ITerminalSessionClaim.description: A terminal claimed by a session, optionally scoped to a specific turn or tool call.

session.description: Session URI that claimed the terminal

turnId.description: Optional turn identifier within the session

toolCallId.description: Optional tool call identifier within the turn

ITerminalState.description: Full state for a single terminal, loaded when a client subscribes to the terminal's URI.

cwd.description: Current working directory of the terminal process

content.description:
Typed content parts, replacing the flat `content: string`.

Naive consumers that only need the raw VT stream can reconstruct it with:
  `content.map(p => p.type === 'command' ? p.output : p.value).join('')`

Consumers that need command boundaries can filter by part type.

exitCode.description: Process exit code, set when the terminal process exits

supportsCommandDetection.description:
Whether this terminal emits `terminal/commandExecuted` and
`terminal/commandFinished` actions and populates `command`-typed parts.

Clients MUST check this flag before relying on command detection.
Do NOT use the presence of a `command` part as a feature flag — parts
are absent in the normal idle state.

ITerminalUnclassifiedPart.description:
Unstructured terminal output — content before, between, or after commands,
or from terminals that do not support command detection.

value.description: Accumulated VT output. Appended to by `terminal/data` when no command is executing.

ITerminalCommandPart.description:
A single command: its command line and the output it produced.

While `isComplete` is false the command is still executing; `output` grows
as `terminal/data` actions arrive. At `terminal/commandFinished` the part
is mutated in-place with `isComplete: true` and the completion metadata.

commandId.description:
Stable id matching the `commandId` on the corresponding
`terminal/commandExecuted` and `terminal/commandFinished` actions.

commandLine.description: The command line submitted to the shell.

output.description:
Accumulated VT output. Appended to by `terminal/data` while `isComplete`
is false. Shell integration escape sequences are stripped by the server.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (ms) when execution started, as reported by the server.

exitCode.description: Shell exit code. Set at completion. `undefined` if unknown.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration in milliseconds. Set at completion.

ISnapshot.description:
A point-in-time snapshot of a subscribed resource's state, returned by
`initialize`, `reconnect`, and `subscribe`.

state.description: The current state of the resource

fromSeq.description: The `serverSeq` at which this snapshot was taken. Subsequent actions will have `serverSeq > fromSeq`.

StringOrMarkdown.description:
A string that may optionally be rendered as Markdown.

- A plain `string` is rendered as-is (no Markdown processing).
- An object with `{ markdown: string }` is rendered with Markdown formatting.

ISessionInputQuestion.description: One question within a session input request.

ISessionInputAnswer.description: Draft, submitted, or skipped answer for one question.

IToolCallState.description:
Discriminated union of all tool call lifecycle states.

See the [state model guide](/guide/state-model.html#tool-call-lifecycle)
for the full state machine diagram.

IToolResultContent.description:
Content block in a tool result.

Mirrors the content blocks in MCP `CallToolResult.content`, plus
`IToolResultResourceContent` for lazy-loading large results,
`IToolResultFileEditContent` for file edit diffs,
`IToolResultTerminalContent` for live terminal output, and
`IToolResultSubagentContent` for subagent sessions (AHP extensions).

ITerminalClaim.description:
Describes who currently holds a terminal. A terminal may be claimed by
either a connected client or a session (e.g. during a tool call).

ITerminalContentPart.description: A content part within terminal output.

IStateAction.description: Discriminated union of all state actions.
